title: Northwind Analytics
preview:
Account summary:
- Northwind Analytics is evaluating Redwood for a performance-sensitive, customer-facing chat experience embedded in their BI product. Primary pain is burst-driven tail latency (p95/p99) when traffic ramps quickly and prompt lengths vary (short chat turns mixed with occasional long context windows). They have strict SLOs and are sensitive to latency spikes more than median latency.

Workload details (from discovery + deep dive):
- Type: chat generation (streaming), some summarization + retrieval-augmented flows.
- Traffic pattern: predictable daily peaks + sharp bursts during “reporting hour”; lots of request cancellations (user interrupts / regenerate) => churn.
- Concurrency target (stated): 250-400 concurrent decode sessions during peak; bursts can exceed this briefly.
- Prompt length mix: most turns small, but intermittent long contexts ("2k-8k tokens") + some long-running sessions.
- Hard KPI they keep repeating: "p99 must stay under ~900ms for first token" during burst, and avoid multi-second outliers. They explicitly asked for p99 and not just p95.

Tail-latency requirements / concerns:
- Their view: sporadic p99 spikes are worse than slightly higher median.
- They suspect KV cache allocator behavior / fragmentation under churn (they referenced issues they've seen in other stacks: OOMs + sudden evictions during peaks).
- Asked directly: "What happens when memory gets tight? Do you shed? Evict? Can we pin premium sessions?"

Deployment + security:
- Must-have: VPC deployment (no public internet path from the runtime). They are okay with a private control plane or customer-managed networking; they asked for a clear diagram of data paths.
- Requirements called out: SSO/SAML for Console access, audit logs (who changed rollout flags), KMS integration, retention controls for logs/traces, and regional data residency (US-only for now).

Product fit notes:
- Strong interest in Dedicated capacity for predictable throughput, but only if it can be delivered with VPC networking constraints; otherwise evaluating Private.
- Asked whether routing/fallback can be configured to avoid “silent model changes” during incidents.
- Wants explicit guardrails around any compaction/eviction feature: ability to disable quickly (kill-switch), monitoring to correlate compaction to latency, and safe defaults by tier.

Linked calls/artifacts:
- Fireflies: 2026-01-14 technical deep dive on tail latency + KV cache behavior; 2026-02-05 POC check-in on stability.
- Gmail threads: 20260109 (priority/rollout alignment), 20260118 (guardrails + rollback authority), 20260207 (escalation follow-up).

Escalations / tickets (informal but tracked like support):
- SUP-28411: Burst tail-latency spikes suspected KV fragmentation; used to coordinate tracing + allocator metrics collection during POC.
- SUP-28433: One intermittent OOM observed during high churn test; they care less about the OOM itself than the “ripple effect” on p99.
- SUP-28472: Latency regression after a canary test in their staging; rolled back and requested clearer automated rollback criteria.

Current status:
- They are still in evaluation. Decision hinges on (1) demonstrating stable p99 under burst with mixed lengths, (2) confirming VPC/private deployment plan, and (3) seeing concrete observability/rollback guardrails for compaction + eviction behavior.

Open questions to resolve:
- Exact definition of their p99 metric (first token vs end-to-end) and whether they measure client-side or server-side.
- Preferred regions + data residency constraints for all logs/metrics.
- Whether they need pinned sessions / tier-aware eviction as a hard requirement for GA.

Internal: owners/hand-offs
- Owner (AE): Nikhil Sharma
- SE: Sean Gallagher (driving SLO framing + guardrails)
- Loop in: Paula Mendes for telemetry-driven rollout policy once VPC plan is locked.

title: Nordic Bank
preview:
Account summary
- Large EU-headquartered bank. Exploring LLM inference vendor for internal customer support + knowledge workflows.
- Primary evaluation driver: EU data residency + strong auditability + ability to report/attribute costs by region/business unit.
- They are skeptical of “black box” managed APIs; want clarity on where data flows and how logs are handled.

Current status (as of 2025-02-22)
- Stage: Evaluation (security review in progress, solution architecture being validated).
- Commercial: early pricing alignment pending (Dedicated EU capacity vs Private VPC deployment).

Key contacts (customer)
- Head of AI Platform (technical sponsor)
- Security Architecture lead (controls, audit logging, SIEM)
- Data Protection Officer team (DPIA/DPA review)
- Finance/Procurement (wants region-specific unit economics; chargeback model)

Use cases / workloads
- Support agent assist (chat + tool calling): internal contact center; expects heavy peak hours.
- Document search + summarization (embeddings + rerank): policy docs, product terms, incident retros.
- Internal email/meeting summarization (batch jobs); strict retention rules.

Technical requirements
- Residency: “EU-only means EU-only” for inference traffic, persistent storage, and observability data. They asked explicitly about:
  - Where request/response logs live
  - Where token metering and cost telemetry are stored
  - Whether support engineers can access payloads (answer: should be no; need redaction + role-based access)
- Network: prefer Private (VPC) with customer-managed networking. Open to Dedicated in EU if control plane + logs can be constrained.
- Identity: SSO/SAML required; SCIM preferred but not a hard requirement for pilot.
- Audit: exportable audit logs (user access, config changes, key usage) to their SIEM.
- Data retention: default “no prompt/response retention”; any operational logs must be configurable with explicit TTL.
- Key management: KMS integration; asked about HSM roadmap (not required for pilot).

Cost / reporting requirements (strong emphasis)
- Need cost visibility by region (EU subregions) + model + deployment tier.
- Finance asked for something like “blended $/1M output tokens by region” so they can allocate spend across business units and compare vendors.
- Quote from security review: “We can’t approve something if we can’t audit it and we can’t allocate it.”
- They asked how we handle idle capacity in Dedicated and how that shows up in cost reporting (i.e., blended vs marginal).
- We referenced internal work on the Unit Economics dashboard (internal-only today) and discussed what we can provide in QBR-style reporting while external dashboards are not productized.

Security review notes (from Fireflies)
- Meeting: 2025-02-14-security-and-data-retention-review-nordicbank
- Topics covered:
  - Data flow: gateway -> runtime -> metering -> warehouse (they want diagram + list of subprocessors)
  - Logs: confirm prompt/response not stored by default; confirm any debugging logs are gated and redacted
  - Retention: configurable TTL; policy must be enforceable; need to document defaults
  - Audit logs: need export mechanism + event types list
  - Incident response: they asked for notification timelines; also asked about past incidents (answer: provide summary + policy)

Recent activity timeline
- 2025-01-29: intro + requirements capture (EU-only + audit/retention called out immediately).
- 2025-02-06: technical deep dive on deployment options (Dedicated EU vs Private VPC).
- 2025-02-14: security & data retention review (Fireflies logged). Action item: provide DPIA starter pack + subprocessor list.
- 2025-02-18: finance follow-up: asked for region-specific cost breakdown and methodology (rate card provenance, idle allocation).
- 2025-02-21: internal: looped in Marcus Lin (security review) + Sean Gallagher (SRE) for SLOs/operational posture; discussed what reporting we can commit to for pilot.

Open questions
- Which EU regions are acceptable for compute + telemetry storage (e.g., eu-north vs eu-west)? They may restrict to specific countries.
- Do they require on-prem in later phase? (They said “not now,” but asked about long-term option.)
- For cost visibility: do they want per-tenant breakdown or just aggregated by business unit? (We must avoid exposing customer-identifiable info in any shared reports.)

Next steps (internal)
- Provide: EU-only architecture diagram + data flow, retention defaults, audit log event catalog.
- Share: cost methodology overview (how $/1M output tokens is computed and reconciled) + note on rate card versioning.
- Schedule: working session with their security architecture team to map controls to our features (RBAC, audit logs, retention, KMS).
- Prep: pilot proposal (success criteria: latency targets, residency validation, audit log export test, cost reporting sample by region/model).

Risks / deal notes
- If we cannot convincingly scope EU-only telemetry + support access controls, risk of security rejection.
- Competitors are pushing “EU region endpoints” but may not satisfy their audit/retention requirements either; we should lean into our Private + observability controls.
- They will likely use region-specific unit economics as a gating criterion during procurement (not just performance).
